title: skopeo sync with src yaml, credentials get ignored in yaml
body:
We want to sync multiple images from nvidia catalog to our own registry using a yaml file a src. but it always ends with an "unauthenticated" error as if the credentials are not used.

```
FATA[0002] Error copying ref "docker://nvcr.io/gpu-operator-3-0:v22.9.1": initializing source docker://nvcr.io/gpu-operator-3-0:v22.9.1: reading manifest v22.9.1 in nvcr.io/gpu-operator-3-0: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
unauthorized: authentication required

```

my yaml file looks like this with one example images we want to sync.

```
nvcr.io:
    images:
        gpu-operator-3-0:
            - "v22.9.1"
    credentials:
        username: $oauthtoken
        password: password
    tls-verify: false


```

this is the command we run:

`skopeo sync --src yaml --dest docker --dest-creds='username':<password> ./sync.yaml my-registry/nvidia`

using src=docker like this, with same user/password combination works without issue.

`skopeo sync --src docker --src-tls-verify=false --src-creds='$oauthtoken':<password> --dest docker --dest-creds='username':<password> nvcr.io/nvaie/gpu-operator-3-0:v22.9.1 my-registry/nvidia`

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

This seems to work as expected for me; and I wouldn’t expect any difference between putting credentials in `--src-creds` and in the YAML file.

Can you paste the log of `skopeo --debug sync …`? I would expect lines like `Returning credentials for nvcr.io/gpu-operator-3-0 from DockerAuthConfig ` in there.

that is what I also would expect. maybe I did something wrong.. already tried to single quote and double quote the password or username but it all doesn't seem to have any positive effekt.

I did a re-run with src=yaml config and with src=docker in debug mode. here is the output.


```
DEBU[0002] GET https://nvcr.io/v2/gpu-operator-3-0/manifests/v22.9.1
```
vs.
```
DEBU[0002] GET https://nvcr.io/v2/nvaie/gpu-operator-3-0/manifests/v22.9.1
```
the two commands specify a different source repository. So something like

```patch
 nvcr.io:
     images:
-        gpu-operator-3-0:
+        nvaie/gpu-operator-3-0:
```
 might make a difference.
you are totally right... choosing the correct repository makes it work like a charm. I should have recognized this by myself earlier.
